comment_text.sn12.40:1.4: This evocative passage is also at Ud 8.4:3.4, MN 144:11.6, and SN 35.87:11.6. | “Inclination” is nati, from a root meaning “to bend”. Sometimes used in the sense of a “inclination of mind” (MN 19:6.1), here, as the following lines make clear, it refers to the mind inclining to rebirth; explained by the commentary as “craving”. Compare the English idiom, “one is bent on destruction”.
comment_text.sn12.40:1.5: The phrase “coming and going” is often used in the context of rebirth (eg. AN 5.57:11.2).

root_text.sn12.40:1.2: “Yañca, bhikkhave, ceteti yañca pakappeti yañca anuseti ārammaṇametaṁ hoti viññāṇassa ṭhitiyā.
root_text.sn12.40:1.4: Tasmiṁ patiṭṭhite viññāṇe virūḷhe nati hoti.
root_text.sn12.40:1.7: Cutūpapāte sati āyatiṁ jāti jarāmaraṇaṁ sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā sambhavanti.
root_text.sn12.40:2.1: No ce, bhikkhave, ceteti no ce pakappeti atha ce anuseti, ārammaṇametaṁ hoti viññāṇassa ṭhitiyā.
root_text.sn12.40:2.3: Tasmiṁ patiṭṭhite viññāṇe virūḷhe nati hoti.
root_text.sn12.40:2.6: Cutūpapāte sati āyatiṁ jāti jarāmaraṇaṁ sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā sambhavanti.
root_text.sn12.40:3.1: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, no ceva ceteti no ca pakappeti no ca anuseti, ārammaṇametaṁ na hoti viññāṇassa ṭhitiyā.
root_text.sn12.40:3.2: Ārammaṇe asati patiṭṭhā viññāṇassa na hoti.
root_text.sn12.40:3.3: Tadappatiṭṭhite viññāṇe avirūḷhe nati na hoti.
root_text.sn12.40:3.6: Cutūpapāte asati āyatiṁ jāti jarāmaraṇaṁ sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā nirujjhanti.

translation_text.sn12.40:1.2: “Mendicants, what you intend or plan, and what you have underlying tendencies for become a support for the continuation of consciousness.
translation_text.sn12.40:1.3: When this support exists, consciousness becomes established.
translation_text.sn12.40:1.4: When consciousness is established and grows, there is an inclination.
translation_text.sn12.40:1.5: When there is an inclination, there is coming and going.
translation_text.sn12.40:1.6: When there is coming and going, there is passing away and reappearing.
translation_text.sn12.40:1.7: When there is passing away and reappearing, future rebirth, old age, and death come to be, as do sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress.
translation_text.sn12.40:1.8: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.40:2.1: If you don’t intend or plan, but still have underlying tendencies, this becomes a support for the continuation of consciousness.
translation_text.sn12.40:2.2: When this support exists, consciousness becomes established.
translation_text.sn12.40:2.3: When consciousness is established and grows, there is an inclination.
translation_text.sn12.40:2.4: When there is an inclination, there is coming and going.
translation_text.sn12.40:2.5: When there is coming and going, there is passing away and reappearing.
translation_text.sn12.40:2.6: When there is passing away and reappearing, future rebirth, old age, and death come to be, as do sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress.
translation_text.sn12.40:2.7: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.40:3.1: If you don’t intend or plan or have underlying tendencies, this doesn’t become a support for the continuation of consciousness.
translation_text.sn12.40:3.2: With no support, consciousness is not established.
translation_text.sn12.40:3.3: When consciousness is not established and doesn’t grow, there’s no inclination.
translation_text.sn12.40:3.4: When there’s no inclination, there’s no coming and going.
translation_text.sn12.40:3.5: When there’s no coming and going, there’s no passing away and reappearing.
translation_text.sn12.40:3.6: When there’s no passing away and reappearing, future rebirth, old age, and death cease, as do sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress.
translation_text.sn12.40:3.7: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.”
